visa_types.name: Cultural and Sports
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/cze/travel-insurance)
Overview 
All supporting documents must be issued no earlier than 6 months (180 days) before the date of filing of appeal to the Visa Service Centre. The visa application can be submitted  not earlier than 6 month prior to the planned trip. Each application must be accompanied by a complete set of documents that match the specified purpose of the trip. In reviewing your visa application, Consulate may request any additional documents if necessary to clarify the circumstances of your trip.
This is to inform you that all documents accompanying the visa application must be up to date and must contain the correct information. In case if there were any changes made to the documents after submission please contact the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg directly.
Russian Law on Data Protection
- According to the Federal law of the Russian Federation №152-ФЗ “On personal data” all applicants should sign “
The agreement on processing personal data (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1kqG7LHSEM9rDfYWJZUEzO/da8e202d46fd6141cbd9805702df4470/consent-new.pdf)”. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should also prepare this document in advance and submit it along with his/her Application.
Please note: Visa servicecentre cannot accept your application in case you don’t fill the data protection form. 
Required documents
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=English&utm_campaign=Czech%20Republic%2FRussia&utm_content=Russian)
To know about the list of  documents you need to submit in support of your visa application please refer to the website of the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg  (https://www.mzv.cz/petersburg/ru/x2001_10_18_1/x2014_02_27/x2014_02_27/index.html )
visa_types.visa_fees:
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service centre to come to the Czech Republic from the Russian Federation.
Dependants are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependants who are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.
Visa Fee and Service Charges
Visa fees may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash Roubles only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
Visa Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Cultural and Sports > Visa Fees 1]
Service Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Cultural and Sports > Visa Fees 2]
Please note:
Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You can use them; you will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee. You must pay this extra fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Please note that all applicants need to pay Service fee.
Visa Fees waived categories
- For close relatives - spouses, children (including adopted) parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren-of citizens of the European Union and of the Russian Federation legally residing in the territory of the Russian Federation or the Member States;
- For members of official delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to the Member States, the European Union or the Russian Federation, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in the territory of the Russian Federation or one of the Member States by intergovernmental organisations;
- Members of national and regional Governments and Parliaments, Constitutional Courts and Supreme Courts, if they are not exempted from the visa requirement by the present Agreement;
- Pupils, full-time course students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary;
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a close relative seriously ill;
- Participants in youth international sports events and persons accompanying them;
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities including university and other exchange programmes;
- Participants in official exchange programmes organized by twin cities
- Child under 6 (excluding express visas)
Note: Please be informed that applying for express visa you will have to pay visa fee even if your application is under one of Visa Fees waived categories. Exception: disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg.
Refund process
The visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded even if your application is refused by the Consulate General of Czech Republic.
Remember to refer Visa applying process
visa_types.processing_time:
Processing times in Russia
The Majority of standard visa applications for nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde are processed starting from 6 working days from the date you submitted your application at the visa application centre. The visa applications of nationals of other countries are processed at least within 15 days. The processing time can be extended up to 45 days.
The Majority of express-visa applications (nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde) are processed from 3 working days from the date you submitted your application (applicable for St.Petersburg).For applications submitted in regional centers please consider the transit time as well.
Important note: Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Czech Republic until you have received your schengen visa. 
The Consulate General of Czech Republic does not guarantee processing of the express-visa within 3-4 working days. 
When to prepare your application
The Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application (https://www.vfsvisaservicesrussia.com/global-passporttracking/track/index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/DZpo5vS2DBzwcXUbPmiXGYm7aaRlB/vrwRKQPzp0pXvuOPvYhgcQwneflYK/JTt/w==) facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service centre by using the details available on the Contact Uspage of this website.
Role of Visa service centre in processing
Please note Visa application centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg.

## Overview
  Description
If you plan to go to Czech Republic to participate in Cultural event or for Sports for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at service center or the Consulate General of Czech Republic, your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must be Czech Republic. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport will show the entry stamp of your first destination only.
General Information
- To know more about Schengen visa and most up to date information we strongly recommend you to refer the Czech Republic website in Russia
- Visa Service centre in Saint Petersburg only accept application from Russian Nationals. All other country nationals must contact the Consulate of Czech Republic directly to apply for the visa.

You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.

## Documents Required

Each application must be accompanied by a complete set of documents that match the specified purpose of the trip. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should also prepare this document in advance and submit it along with his/her Application.

To know about the list of  documents you need to submit in support of your visa application please refer to the website of the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg  (https://www.mzv.cz/petersburg/ru/x2001_10_18_1/x2014_02_27/x2014_02_27/index.html )

## Photo Specifications
- Overview (#1)
- Photo Requirements (#2)
- More Information (#4)

## Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 1 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.

## Photo Requirements
Your visa application photo  must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that  do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application process. Your  photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured  from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must  be between 70% to 80% of the photo's total height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3.5cm x  4.5cm.

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Bho7jtJCS8sdYJsJ7OJyT/fe4942ed7df83e7cc4460c2743f78339/annex-a-photograph-guidelines-english-260315.pdf)
If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Processing times in Russia
The Majority of standard visa applications for nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde are processed starting from 6 working days from the date you submitted your application at the visa application centre. The visa applications of nationals of other countries are processed at least within 15 days. The processing time can be extended up to 45 days.
The Majority of express-visa applications (nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde) are processed from 3 working days from the date you submitted your application (applicable for St.Petersburg).For applications submitted in regional centers please consider the transit time as well.
Important note: Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Czech Republic until you have received your schengen visa. 
The Consulate General of Czech Republic does not guarantee processing of the express-visa within 3-4 working days. 
When to prepare your application
The Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application (https://www.vfsvisaservicesrussia.com/global-passporttracking/track/index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/DZpo5vS2DBzwcXUbPmiXGYm7aaRlB/vrwRKQPzp0pXvuOPvYhgcQwneflYK/JTt/w==) facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service centre by using the details available on the Contact Uspage of this website.
Role of Visa service centre in processing
Please note Visa application centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg.

## Download Form
Dear applicants, please note that in each visa application center you can opt for additional service of completing visa application forms by professional staff. The service is 300 rubles per application form, and you can opt for it even if you are not going to submit you documents for a visa in this particular visa application center.
This page hosts the forms required for  your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa  application form. 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5UphmrtCDYUQO2ny0m4PSb/4003d63d302f85aea5398a0ecd08fdc2/visa-application-form-cz.pdf)
Sample Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7dpw5faARedHv7WJvU6GcK/af76aef1b6e99712829ab230279a5ea3/visa-application-form-sample.pdf)

## How to fill visa application form
Information  for applicants: Application  form is being read and processed electronically Your application may be delayed  or not getting processed, in case the application form is filled incorrectly or  false information is provided. 
Detailed instructions for completing the application form section (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/17S3CcyvnFJiJrUdOvXLfy/088d9deab45c01f28104e56eea91b987/visa-aplication-form-instruction.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.

Head Size

This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service centre to come to the Czech Republic from the Russian Federation.
Dependants are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependants who are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.
Visa Fee and Service Charges
Visa fees may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash Roubles only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
Visa Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Education or Scientific Research > Visa Fees 1]
Service Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Education or Scientific Research > Visa Fees 2]
Please note:
Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You can use them; you will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee. You must pay this extra fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Please note that all applicants need to pay Service fee.
Visa Fees waived categories
- For close relatives - spouses, children (including adopted) parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren-of citizens of the European Union and of the Russian Federation legally residing in the territory of the Russian Federation or the Member States;
- For members of official delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to the Member States, the European Union or the Russian Federation, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in the territory of the Russian Federation or one of the Member States by intergovernmental organisations;
- Members of national and regional Governments and Parliaments, Constitutional Courts and Supreme Courts, if they are not exempted from the visa requirement by the present Agreement;
- Pupils, full-time course students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary;
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a close relative seriously ill;
- Participants in youth international sports events and persons accompanying them;
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities including university and other exchange programmes;
- Participants in official exchange programmes organized by twin cities
- Child under 6 (excluding express visas)
Note: Please be informed that applying for express visa you will have to pay visa fee even if your application is under one of Visa Fees waived categories. Exception: disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg.
Refund process
The visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded even if your application is refused by the Consulate General of Czech Republic.
Remember to refer Visa applying process

Dependants are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependants who are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.
Visa Fee and Service Charges (#1)
Optional services (#2)
Visa Fees waived categories (#3)
Refund process (#4)

## Visa Fee and Service Charges
Visa fees may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash Roubles only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
Visa Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Property owners > Visa Fees 1]
Service Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Property owners > Visa Fees 2]
Please note:
Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You can use them; you will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee. You must pay this extra fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Please note that all applicants need to pay Service fee.

## Visa Fees waived categories
- For close relatives - spouses, children (including adopted) parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren-of citizens of the European Union and of the Russian Federation legally residing in the territory of the Russian Federation or the Member States;
- For members of official delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to the Member States, the European Union or the Russian Federation, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in the territory of the Russian Federation or one of the Member States by intergovernmental organisations;
- Members of national and regional Governments and Parliaments, Constitutional Courts and Supreme Courts, if they are not exempted from the visa requirement by the present Agreement;
- Pupils, full-time course students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary;
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a close relative seriously ill;
- Participants in youth international sports events and persons accompanying them;
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities including university and other exchange programmes;
- Participants in official exchange programmes organized by twin cities
- Child under 6 (excluding express visas)
Note: Please be informed that applying for express visa you will have to pay visa fee even if your application is under one of Visa Fees waived categories. Exception: disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg.

## Refund process
The visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded even if your application is refused by the Consulate General of Czech Republic.
Remember to refer Visa applying process

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/cze/travel-insurance)
All supporting documents must be issued no earlier than 6 months (180 days) before the date of filing of appeal to the Visa Service Centre. The visa application can be submitted  not earlier than 6 month prior to the planned trip. Each application must be accompanied by a complete set of documents that match the specified purpose of the trip. In reviewing your visa application, Consulate may request any additional documents if necessary to clarify the circumstances of your trip.
This is to inform you that all documents accompanying the visa application must be up to date and must contain the correct information. In case if there were any changes made to the documents after submission please contact the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg directly.
Russian Law on Data Protection
- According to the Federal law of the Russian Federation №152-ФЗ “On personal data” all applicants should sign “
The agreement on processing personal data (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1kqG7LHSEM9rDfYWJZUEzO/da8e202d46fd6141cbd9805702df4470/consent-new.pdf)”. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should also prepare this document in advance and submit it along with his/her Application.
Please note: Visa  service centre cannot accept your application in case you don’t fill the data protection form.
Required documents
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=English&utm_campaign=Czech%20Republic%2FRussia&utm_content=Russian)
To know about the list of  documents you need to submit in support of your visa application please refer to the website of the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg  (https://www.mzv.cz/petersburg/ru/x2001_10_18_1/x2014_02_27/x2014_02_27/index.html )

## Photo Specifications
- Overview (#a)
- Photo Requirements (#b)
- More Information (#c)

## Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 1 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa application.

## Photo Requirements
Your visa application photo  must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that  do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application process. Your  photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured  from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must  be between 70% to 80% of the photo's total height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3.5cm x  4.5cm.

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Bho7jtJCS8sdYJsJ7OJyT/fe4942ed7df83e7cc4460c2743f78339/annex-a-photograph-guidelines-english-260315.pdf)
If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Processing times in Russia
The Majority of standard visa applications for nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde are processed starting from 6 working days from the date you submitted your application at the visa application centre. The visa applications of nationals of other countries are processed at least within 15 days. The processing time can be extended up to 45 days.
The Majority of express-visa applications (nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde) are processed from 3 working days from the date you submitted your application (applicable for St.Petersburg). For applications submitted in regional centers please consider the transit time as well.
Important note: Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Czech Republic until you have received your schengen visa. 
The Consulate General of Czech Republic does not guarantee processing of the express-visa within 3-4 working days. 
When to prepare your application
The Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application (https://www.vfsvisaservicesrussia.com/global-passporttracking/track/index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/DZpo5vS2DBzwcXUbPmiXGYm7aaRlB/vrwRKQPzp0pXvuOPvYhgcQwneflYK/JTt/w==) facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service centre by using the details available on the Contact Uspage of this website.
Role of Visa service centre in processing
Please note Visa application centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg.

## Download Form
Dear applicants, please note that in each visa application center you can opt for additional service of completing visa application forms by professional staff. The service is 300 rubles per application form, and you can opt for it even if you are not going to submit you documents for a visa in this particular visa application center.
This page hosts the forms required for  your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa  application form. 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5UphmrtCDYUQO2ny0m4PSb/4003d63d302f85aea5398a0ecd08fdc2/visa-application-form-cz.pdf)
Sample Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7dpw5faARedHv7WJvU6GcK/af76aef1b6e99712829ab230279a5ea3/visa-application-form-sample.pdf)

## How to fill visa application form
Information  for applicants: Application  form is being read and processed electronically Your application may be delayed  or not getting processed, in case the application form is filled incorrectly or  false information is provided. 
Detailed instructions for completing the application form section (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/17S3CcyvnFJiJrUdOvXLfy/088d9deab45c01f28104e56eea91b987/visa-aplication-form-instruction.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
  Description 
If you plan to go to Czech Republic as a Close relative of EU Citizen for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at service centre or the Consulate General of Czech Republic, your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must be Czech Republic. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport will show the entry stamp of your first destination only
General Information
- To know more about Schengen visa and most up to date information we strongly recommend you to refer the Czech Republic website in Russia.
- Visa Service centre in Saint Petersburg only accept application from Russian Nationals. All other country nationals must contact the Consulate of Czech Republic directly to apply for the visa.

## Overview
If you plan to go to Czech Republic for work for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at service centre or the Consulate General of Czech Republic, your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must be Czech Republic. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport will show the entry stamp of your first destination only 
General Information
- To know more about Schengen visa and most up to date information we strongly recommend you to refer the Czech Republic website in Russia.
- Visa Service centre in Saint Petersburg only accept application from Russian Nationals. All other country nationals must contact the Consulate of Czech Republic directly to apply for the visa.

## Visa Fees
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service centre to come to the Czech Republic from the Russian Federation.
Dependants are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependants who are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.
Visa Fee and Service Charges (#1)
Optional services (#2)
Visa Fees waived categories (#3)
Refund process (#4)

## Visa Fee and Service Charges
Visa fees may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash Roubles only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
Visa Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Work > Visa Fees 1]
Service Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Work > Visa Fees 2]
Please note:
Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You can use them; you will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee. You must pay this extra fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Please note that all applicants need to pay Service fee.

## Visa Fees waived categories
- For close relatives - spouses, children (including adopted) parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren-of citizens of the European Union and of the Russian Federation legally residing in the territory of the Russian Federation or the Member States;
- For members of official delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to the Member States, the European Union or the Russian Federation, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in the territory of the Russian Federation or one of the Member States by intergovernmental organisations;
- Members of national and regional Governments and Parliaments, Constitutional Courts and Supreme Courts, if they are not exempted from the visa requirement by the present Agreement;
- Pupils, full-time course students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary;
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a close relative seriously ill;
- Participants in youth international sports events and persons accompanying them;
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities including university and other exchange programmes;
- Participants in official exchange programmes organized by twin cities
- Child under 6 (excluding express visas)
Note: Please be informed that applying for express visa you will have to pay visa fee even if your application is under one of Visa Fees waived categories. Exception: disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg.

## Refund process
The visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded even if your application is refused by the Consulate General of Czech Republic.
Remember to refer Visa applying process

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/cze/travel-insurance)
       Overview 
All supporting documents must be issued no earlier than 6 months (180 days) before the date of filing of appeal to the Visa Service Centre. The visa application can be submitted  not earlier than 6 month prior to the planned trip. Each application must be accompanied by a complete set of documents that match the specified purpose of the trip. In reviewing your visa application, Consulate may request any additional documents if necessary to clarify the circumstances of your trip.
This is to inform you that all documents accompanying the visa application must be up to date and must contain the correct information. In case if there were any changes made to the documents after submission please contact the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg directly.
Russian Law on Data Protection
- According to the Federal law of the Russian Federation №152-ФЗ “On personal data” all applicants should sign “
The agreement on processing personal data (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1kqG7LHSEM9rDfYWJZUEzO/da8e202d46fd6141cbd9805702df4470/consent-new.pdf)”. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should also prepare this document in advance and submit it along with his/her Application.
Please note: Visa service centre cannot accept your application in case you don’t fill the data protection form.
Required documents
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=English&utm_campaign=Czech%20Republic%2FRussia&utm_content=Russian)
To know about the list of  documents you need to submit in support of your visa application please refer to the website of the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg  (https://www.mzv.cz/petersburg/ru/x2001_10_18_1/x2014_02_27/x2014_02_27/index.html )

## Photo Specifications
Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 1 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.
Photo Requirements
Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the image below:
Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 70% to 80% of the photo's total height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3.5cm x 4.5cm. 
More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Bho7jtJCS8sdYJsJ7OJyT/fe4942ed7df83e7cc4460c2743f78339/annex-a-photograph-guidelines-english-260315.pdf)
If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our visa application centre.

Processing times in Russia
The Majority of standard visa applications for nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde are processed starting from 6 working days from the date you submitted your application at the visa application centre. The visa applications of nationals of other countries are processed at least within 15 days. The processing time can be extended up to 45 days.
The Majority of express-visa applications (nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde) are processed from 3 working days from the date you submitted your application (applicable for St.Petersburg).For applications submitted in regional centers please consider the transit time as well.
Important note: Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Czech Republic until you have received your schengen visa. 
The Consulate General of Czech Republic does not guarantee processing of the express-visa within 3-4 working days. 
When to prepare your application
The Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application (https://www.vfsvisaservicesrussia.com/global-passporttracking/track/index?q=shSA0YnE4pLF9Xzwon/x/DZpo5vS2DBzwcXUbPmiXGYm7aaRlB/vrwRKQPzp0pXvuOPvYhgcQwneflYK/JTt/w==) facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service centre by using the details available on the Contact Uspage of this website.
Role of Visa service centre in processing
Please note Visa application centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the General Consulate of Czech Republic in Saint-Petersburg.

## Download Form
Dear applicants, please note that in each visa application center you can opt for additional service of completing visa application forms by professional staff. The service is 300 rubles per application form, and you can opt for it even if you are not going to submit you documents for a visa in this particular visa application center.
This page hosts the forms required for  your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa  application form. 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5UphmrtCDYUQO2ny0m4PSb/4003d63d302f85aea5398a0ecd08fdc2/visa-application-form-cz.pdf)
Sample Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7dpw5faARedHv7WJvU6GcK/af76aef1b6e99712829ab230279a5ea3/visa-application-form-sample.pdf)

## How to fill visa application form
Information  for applicants: Application  form is being read and processed electronically Your application may be delayed  or not getting processed, in case the application form is filled incorrectly or  false information is provided. 
Detailed instructions for completing the application form section (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/17S3CcyvnFJiJrUdOvXLfy/088d9deab45c01f28104e56eea91b987/visa-aplication-form-instruction.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.

Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You can use them; you will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee. You must pay this extra fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Please note that all applicants need to pay Service fee.

## Visa Fees waived categories
- For close relatives - spouses, children (including adopted) parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren-of citizens of the European Union and of the Russian Federation legally residing in the territory of the Russian Federation or the Member States;
- For members of official delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to the Member States, the European Union or the Russian Federation, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in the territory of the Russian Federation or one of the Member States by intergovernmental organisations;
- Members of national and regional Governments and Parliaments, Constitutional Courts and Supreme Courts, if they are not exempted from the visa requirement by the present Agreement;

- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a close relative seriously ill;
- Participants in youth international sports events and persons accompanying them;
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities including university and other exchange programmes;
- Participants in official exchange programmes organized by twin cities
- Child under 6 (excluding express visas)
Note: Please be informed that applying for express visa you will have to pay visa fee even if your application is under one of Visa Fees waived categories. Exception: disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary.

The visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund

## Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 1 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.

## Photo Requirements
Your visa application photo  must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that  do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application process. Your  photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured  from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must  be between 70% to 80% of the photo's total height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3.5cm x  4.5cm.

If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Processing times in Russia
The Majority of standard visa applications for nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde are processed starting from 6 working days from the date you submitted your application at the visa application centre. The visa applications of nationals of other countries are processed at least within 15 days. The processing time can be extended up to 45 days.
The Majority of express-visa applications (nationals of Russia, Georgia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Azerbaijan and Cabo Verde) are processed from 3 working days from the date you submitted your application (applicable for St.Petersburg). For applications submitted in regional centers please consider the transit time as well.
Important note: Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Czech Republic until you have received your schengen visa.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Description 
 If you plan to go to Czech Republic as a tourist or for Treatment for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at service centre or the Consulate General of Czech Republic, your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must be Czech Republic. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport will show the entry stamp of your first destination only 
General Information
- To know more about Schengen visa and most up to date information we strongly recommend you to refer the Czech Republic website in Russia.

Dependants are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependants who are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.

Optional services (#2)
Visa Fees waived categories (#3)

## Visa Fee and Service Charges
Visa fees may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash Roubles only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
Visa Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Tourism or Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
Service Charges:

[cze > rus > en > st-petersburg > Table:Tourism or Treatment > Visa Fees 2]
Please note:
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.

## Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You can use them; you will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee. You must pay this extra fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website. 
Please note that all applicants need to pay Service fee.

## Visa Fees waived categories
- For close relatives - spouses, children (including adopted) parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren-of citizens of the European Union and of the Russian Federation legally residing in the territory of the Russian Federation or the Member States;
- For members of official delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to the Member States, the European Union or the Russian Federation, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in the territory of the Russian Federation or one of the Member States by intergovernmental organisations;
- Members of national and regional Governments and Parliaments, Constitutional Courts and Supreme Courts, if they are not exempted from the visa requirement by the present Agreement;
- Pupils, full-time course students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary;
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a close relative seriously ill;
- Participants in youth international sports events and persons accompanying them;
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities including university and other exchange programmes;
- Participants in official exchange programmes organized by twin cities
- Child under 6 (excluding express visas)
Note: Please be informed that applying for express visa you will have to pay visa fee even if your application is under one of Visa Fees waived categories. Exception: disabled person and one accompanying person, if necessary.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consulate General of Czech Republic in Saint Petersburg.

## Refund process
The visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded even if your application is refused by the Consulate General of Czech Republic.
